nodes.title: ② Depth Layer Mask — bg band, fill_occluded (occlusion_mask drives the external inpaint)
③ Clean Plate — background, fill_occluded=True (plate placeholder: swap for inpaint chain)
④ Clean Plate — foreground, original pixels (nothing occludes it)

widgets_values:
ATLAS COMPLETE DMP PIPELINE — the full matte-painting story in one graph (sky dome + depth-layered clean plates + disocclusion fill + true depth-shadow occlusion)

This is the capstone example tying together the whole Atlas Camera inpaint/projection system:

1. SKY: a real segmentation (placeholder SolidMask here — swap for ComfyUI-RMBG's SAM3 Segmentation prompted 'sky') separates the sky onto AtlasSkyDomeLayer's flat far card, clean-plated and projected independently of noisy monocular sky depth.
2. DEPTH LAYERS: AtlasDepthLayerMask splits the solved photo into metric bands; its occlusion_mask drives an external inpaint (placeholder: original photo wired directly — swap for INPAINT_ExpandMask -> INPAINT_InpaintWithModel per INSTALL.md) to build each band's clean plate; AtlasCleanPlateLayer projects each plate onto its own band-clipped relief mesh.
3. DISOCCLUSION FILL (fill_occluded=True, new): the band clip normally leaves a hole in the background mesh exactly where the foreground occluder stood — the inpainted plate had pixels there but no geometry. fill_occluded diffusion-fills the occluder footprint's depth from the surrounding background, so orbiting now reveals INPAINTED CONTENT ON REAL GEOMETRY instead of a hole. n_filled_cells is recorded in the layer's metadata; AtlasDepthLayerMask's matching fill_occluded keeps its pre-inpaint hole_mask in lockstep.
4. DEPTH-SHADOW OCCLUSION (occlusion_mode=depth_shadow, new): AtlasOcclusionMask now detects true MPTK-style self-occlusion — surfaces hidden behind NEARER geometry from the primary's view — by treating the primary camera as a light and its own AtlasDepthMap as the shadow map (no render pass, pure numpy). Wire primary_depth from the same shared AtlasDepthMap. The bottom-left corner demos it; its target_image placeholder is the original photo — swap for a real Qwen Multiple-Angles novel view, then feed the composited result into AtlasAddPatchView per the multi-angle patch pipeline.

Run it, open the Atlas Viewport, hit Project, then orbit/dolly: sky stays clean on its card, depth layers separate in parallax, and the previously-black disocclusion hole behind the foreground now shows the (placeholder) plate on synthesized geometry.

See CLAUDE.md's 'Inpaint layers', 'Multi-angle patch projection', and 'Relief mesh tearing' key design rules.

--- MASTER PIPELINE ADDITIONS (this workflow) ---
5. QWEN MULTI-ANGLE PATCH, angle-driven by the viewport: run the graph once, open the Atlas Viewport, orbit/fly to the view your camera move should end on, click 📐 Extract Angle, then Queue again. The viewport's patch_prompt output feeds the Qwen Image Edit 2511 subgraph directly (replacing the manual QwenMultiangleCameraNode angle picker), and patch_azimuth_view/patch_elevation_view/patch_distance feed AtlasAddPatchView's converted widget-inputs - the SAME snapped angles everywhere, no retyping, exact round-trip through orbit_camera. Leave flip_azimuth OFF for extracted angles.
6. NUKE LAYERS EXPORT (AtlasExportNukeLayers): every projection layer on the final solve - sky dome, bg/fg clean plates, the Qwen patch - exports as ONE native .nk: per-layer Read + Camera2 + Project3D2 + ReadGeo2 (OBJ written alongside), merged through a single Scene into one ScanlineRender rendered from the primary camera. Drag-and-drop into Nuke; layer overlap resolves by real z-depth.

AUTOMATIC PAUSE/RESUME: until 📐 Extract Angle has been clicked, the viewport's patch_* outputs return ComfyUI's ExecutionBlocker - the whole patch branch (Qwen generation, AtlasAddPatchView, final viewport, Nuke layers export) is silently SKIPPED on the first Queue instead of wasting a zero-orbit generation. Orbit/fly to your target view, click 📐 Extract Angle, and the branch resumes by itself (the button re-queues automatically) with the extracted views flowing everywhere. Requires the qwen_image_edit_2511 models + both LoRAs from the atlas_qwen_image_edit_2511_multiangle_camera.json example (see its subgraph for exact filenames).

7. VLM ASSESSMENT GATE (AtlasAssessImage, head of the graph): a local VLM (ollama gemma3:4b by default; LM Studio/llama.cpp also supported) analyzes the photo against Atlas's full settings knowledge and writes a setup plan to the Show Text node - scene type, depth model, band splits, sky prompt, relief tuning, camera-move viability (score + max orbit degrees), patch recommendations. The graph is PAUSED at this node (only the assessment runs on first Queue); read the report, apply the recommended settings, then click ▶ Continue Workflow on the node. The assessment is cached per image, so continuing doesn't re-run the VLM. No VLM server running? The report says so and ▶ Continue still works - the advisor is optional, never a hard dependency. Requires pythongosssss custom-scripts for the Show Text node (or wire report into any text display).

8. MAYA LAYERS EXPORT (AtlasExportMayaLayers): the Maya twin of the Nuke export - every layer as ONE .ma: native per-layer projector cameras + an on-open scriptNode that imports the OBJs and builds the camera-projection shading networks (projection node projType 8, place3dTexture under each layer's camera). Edge mattes ride the plate alpha into lambert transparency. Same assets on disk as the Nuke export (plates/mattes/OBJs). If Maya's script security blocks scriptNodes, import the OBJs manually - they sit next to the .ma.

nodes.title: ① Sky Dome Layer — flat far card
bg hole_mask (fill_occluded — occluder footprint is NOT a hole)
Blockout — Shaded
depth_shadow occlusion — white = hidden from the primary, patch fills it
⑥ Occlusion Mask — depth_shadow (target placeholder: swap for a Qwen novel view)
⑤ Viewport — hit Project, orbit into the disocclusion
Qwen Image Edit 2511 + Multiple-Angles LoRA (prompt = viewport patch_prompt)
Generated novel view (patch)
Add Patch View — angles wired from the viewport's Extract Angle
FINAL viewport — sky + layers + patch composited (hit Project, orbit)
Nuke Layers Export — ALL layers in one .nk
🧭 Assess Image — VLM setup advisor (PAUSES the graph until ▶ Continue)
Assessment report — read, apply settings, then ▶ Continue Workflow
Maya Layers Export — ALL layers in one .ma
SAM3 on the PATCH image — sky out of the patch mesh

groups.title: ② Sky Dome (placeholder mask — see Note)
③ Depth Layers + Disocclusion Fill (placeholder plates — see Note)
④ Viewport — sky + layers + filled disocclusion composited
⑤ True Depth-Shadow Occlusion (MPTK camera-as-light) — multi-angle patch pipeline
⑥ Qwen Multi-Angle Patch — prompt driven by 📐 Extract Angle
⑦ Patched Result + DCC Layer Exports (one .nk + one .ma, all layers)
⓪ VLM Pre-flight — assess, apply settings, then continue

nodes.title: TextEncodeQwenImageEditPlus (Positive)
widgets_values: Change the furniture leather difference in image 1 to the fur material in image 2.

nodes.title: KSampler settings
widgets_values:
You can test and find the best setting by yourself. The following table is for reference.
|   | Qwen | Comfy | lightning LoRA |
|--------|---------|------------|---------------------------|
| Steps  | 40      | 20         | 4                         |
| CFG    | 4.0     | 4.0       | 1.0                       |

By default, we use 20 steps as we just don't want it to take too long. Try 40 if you want a better result, but it will take longer.
